Yes — I pulled the latest dosing parameters from `helios_dosing_params_v3.yaml` (last updated **2026-03-28**).

**Quick dosing summary for Dr. Martinez**

| Patient | Age | Weight | Dose | Calculated dose* | Frequency | Next dose | PK clearance | Notes |
|---|---:|---:|---:|---:|---|---|---:|---|
| CU-001 | 34 mo | 12.4 kg | 0.15 mg/kg | 1.86 mg | q3w | 2026-04-14 | 3.2 mL/min | Stable; tumor regression 22% at last scan |
| CU-002 | 18 mo | 9.1 kg | 0.12 mg/kg | 1.09 mg | q3w | 2026-04-12 | 2.8 mL/min | Responding well; weight gain improving |
| CU-003 | 51 mo | 15.7 kg | 0.18 mg/kg | 2.83 mg | q2w | 2026-04-10 | 4.1 mL/min | New enrollment; first dose given 2026-03-27 |

\*Calculated as weight × dose mg/kg; final pharmacy prep may still require site-specific rounding.

**PK model status**
- From the dosing config, nothing looks obviously out of line: all three patients have individualized clearance values, dosing schedules, and upcoming administration dates.
- That said, this file alone is **not** a live stability check on the PK models, so I can’t fully confirm runtime/model health just from the config.

If you want, I can format this into a short report draft for forwarding to Dr. Martinez.
